title: Conveyor C-12 Bearing Failure — TFB Line B
description: Operators reported grinding noise and vibration on conveyor C-12 at start of day shift. Vibration measurement: 6.8 mm/s RMS (Zone C per STD-010). Line stopped 47 minutes for emergency bearing replacement.
root_cause: Bearing WO-7821 lubrication PM was overdue by 23 days. Grease fitting on drive-side bearing was blocked — zero grease reached the bearing over last two PM cycles.
resolution: Replaced both drive-side and idler-side bearings (SKF 6205-2RS). Cleared grease fitting, verified flow. Rescheduled PM from 90-day to 60-day cycle for C-12 specifically. Returned to production after first-article inspection.

title: Conveyor C-12 — Repeat Vibration Event
description: Second vibration event on C-12 within 90 days. Vibration at 5.2 mm/s RMS (Zone C). No noise this time; caught by routine predictive check. Bearing condition: minor pitting on outer race, not yet failed.
root_cause: PM interval reset to 60 days after INC-2024-001 but not updated in CMMS — technician still following 90-day schedule. Lubrication resumed on 90-day cadence; grease depleted before next PM.
resolution: Proactive bearing replacement. CMMS PM record corrected to 60-day cycle. Supervisor sign-off required on all C-12 PM completion records going forward.

title: Conveyor C-12 — Third Bearing Event (Belt Damage)
description: Third bearing-related stoppage on C-12. This time the bearing failed fully — seized drive-side bearing caused belt slippage and edge fraying. Output loss: ~200 units over 2-hour stoppage.
root_cause: Bearing replacement parts quality: maintenance sourced non-OEM replacement (SKF equivalent from distributor) in January; lot trace reveals under-spec inner diameter on two of six bearings purchased. Two failed within one year vs expected 3+ year MTBF.
resolution: Replaced bearing with verified OEM SKF 6205-2RS. Belt section replaced. Supplier quality alert issued for non-OEM bearing lot. STD-005 incoming inspection added for bearing lots.

title: SAP Line A Press P-3 — Burr Height Exceedance
description: Quality hold triggered at 14:30: 8 of 25 sampled parts showed burr height 0.15–0.22 mm (limit 0.1 mm per STD-009). Approximately 400 parts in suspect lot.
root_cause: Die clearance set to 8% of material thickness by setup tech; traveler specified 10% ±0.5%. Setup traveler was rev-level B; press team had rev A (two revisions behind). Document control failure.
resolution: 400-part lot quarantined and sorted (212 scrap, 188 rework-passed). Die reset to 10% clearance, first-piece confirmed. Document control issued ECN to retire rev A travelers. All press setup techs re-trained.

title: SAP Line B — 22% Throughput Drop, Conveyor C-08 Jam
description: Throughput dropped 22% over 40 minutes starting 07:15. Root operator reported unusual noise from C-08. Jam cleared manually by maintenance without LOTO — safety incident logged separately.
root_cause: Foreign object (bolt fragment) lodged in conveyor drive sprocket. Object source traced to upstream assembly station where bolt torque operation was not contained.
resolution: Object removed, drive sprocket inspected (no damage). FOD (Foreign Object Debris) containment added to upstream torque station. LOTO violation documented; maintenance tech retrained.

title: BPK Line 1 — Seal Integrity Failures, Quality Hold
description: Destructive peel test at 10:00 found 3 of 5 samples failed peel strength (<12 N/15mm). Seal failure rate calculated at 1.8% (limit 0.5% per STD-012). Line stopped. ~1,200 units on hold.
root_cause: Seal bar SB-1 thermocouple drift: actual temp 128°C vs setpoint 150°C. Thermocouple calibration was 7 months overdue (12-month PM schedule). Low seal temp caused inadequate polymer bond.
resolution: Thermocouple replaced and calibrated. All held units 100% inspected (780 pass, 420 scrap). PM schedule for seal bar thermocouples changed to 6-month cycle.

title: SAP Line C — Supplier Lot Rejection (PPAP Failure)
description: Incoming lot from supplier Jenner Valve (supplier ID: SUP-JV) failed PPAP re-verification. Cpk for bore diameter: 1.19 (minimum 1.33 for conditional approval per STD-011). Entire lot quarantined.
root_cause: Supplier changed machining center without notifying customer. New machine tool had higher tool wear rate — dimensions drifting over the lot. No PPAP notification submitted.
resolution: Lot rejected, returned to supplier. SCAR-2024-007 issued (second SCAR in 12 months for SUP-JV). Supplier put on conditional approval; all incoming lots require 100% dimensional inspection until corrective action closed.

title: SAP Line C — Second Supplier Lot Rejection (Same Supplier)
description: Another Jenner Valve lot rejected at incoming inspection. Cpk for bore diameter: 1.21. This is the third SCAR for SUP-JV in 12 months, triggering mandatory supplier audit per STD-005.
root_cause: Supplier's corrective action from SCAR-2024-007 was inadequate — statistical process control was not implemented on the machining center as promised. Tool wear still not monitored.
resolution: Lot rejected. SCAR-2024-021 issued (third in 12 months). Supplier audit scheduled for Q4 2024. Emergency sourcing from backup supplier SUP-DM initiated.

title: BPK Line 2 — Sanitation Skip, Quality Hold
description: Line 2 restarted after weekend idle (63 hours) without performing pre-production sanitation per STD-004. Discovered by QA auditor on Monday morning walk. ATP swab result: 2,400 RLU (limit 100 RLU). ~8 hours of production on quality hold.
root_cause: Shift supervisor believed Friday's sanitation (pre-shutdown) covered the restart. Procedure requires sanitation if idle >8 hours, regardless of pre-shutdown clean. Gap in procedure training.
resolution: All production from restart to discovery (approx. 12,000 units) placed on quality hold. Full sanitation performed; ATP confirmed <100 RLU. QA retained 2,000 units for microbiological testing (results: pass). Remaining 10,000 units released after 72-hour hold. Shift supervisor and all line operators retrained on STD-004.

title: TFB Line 1 — Press P-1 Mechanical Failure, Near-Miss
description: Press P-1 upper ram dropped unexpectedly during tool change. Technician's hand was in the die space — pulled back in time, no injury. Near-miss classified as High Potential. LOTO was applied but pneumatic counterbalance pressure was not bled off.
root_cause: LOTO procedure for P-1 did not explicitly list the pneumatic counterbalance as an energy source to isolate. Energy isolation checklist was incomplete.
resolution: Press taken out of service. EHS conducted investigation. P-1 LOTO procedure revised to include pneumatic counterbalance bleed-off as mandatory step. All press operators and maintenance techs on TFB retrained. Press returned to service after revised LOTO verified.

title: SAP Line A — Throughput Drop 18%, Conveyor C-03
description: Throughput on SAP Line A dropped 18% starting 06:00 shift start. Two operators reported vibration on conveyor C-03. No noise initially. Belt speed normal on HMI readout.
root_cause: Bearing outer race spalling detected on C-03 drive bearing during maintenance inspection triggered by vibration report. Vibration at 5.8 mm/s RMS (Zone C). PM for C-03 bearing had been completed 45 days prior — within schedule but bearing sourced from same non-OEM lot as C-12 failures (INC-2024-003).
resolution: C-03 drive bearing replaced with verified OEM SKF part. Full audit of non-OEM bearing inventory initiated. Seven additional bearings from suspect lot replaced proactively on SAP and TFB lines.

title: TFB Line 2 — SPC Out-of-Control Signal, Part Flatness
description: SPC control chart for part flatness on Press P-4 showed 9 consecutive points above mean (Nelson Rule 4) starting at 10:15. Process not stopped for 52 minutes after signal (limit 30 min per STD-006).
root_cause: Die temperature drift — ambient temp in building climbed to 38°C during heat event. Thermal expansion of die components shifting flatness. Also: SPC chart was being monitored by operator, not quality tech — operator was unaware of Nelson Rule 4.
resolution: Added portable cooling to die area. Die temperature log added to process traveler. SPC monitoring responsibility clarified: quality tech reviews every 30 min. Operator training on Nelson rules added to annual qualification.

title: BPK Line 3 — Conveyor CB-5 Drive Motor Overload
description: CB-5 drive motor tripped overload relay at 13:45. Motor running at 142°C winding temp (limit 130°C). Line stopped, 35-minute downtime.
root_cause: Product accumulation on CB-5 caused by intermittent jam on downstream packaging station. Motor drawing excess current while pushing accumulated product. Accumulation sensor had failed — no auto-stop triggered.
resolution: Motor cooled and reset. Downstream jam cleared (label roll empty — operator had not stocked backup rolls). Accumulation sensor replaced. Added check of backup label stock to shift startup checklist.

title: SAP Line B — Raw Material Quality Hold (Dimensional)
description: Incoming lot from supplier Performa Metals (SUP-PM) failed AQL inspection: 4 of 50 sampled parts out of tolerance on wall thickness (limit ±0.05 mm; nonconforming measured 0.09–0.12 mm oversize). Lot quarantined.
root_cause: Supplier experiencing tool wear issue on forming operation; did not perform incoming SPC before shipment. First SCAR for SUP-PM.
resolution: Lot returned. SCAR-2024-019 issued. 100% inspection required on next two lots from SUP-PM. Supplier committed to SPC implementation within 30 days.

title: BPK Line 1 — Seal Bar SB-2 Temperature Runaway
description: Seal bar SB-2 temperature controller failed high: temperature climbed to 198°C (setpoint 150°C). High-limit cutout activated. ~80 units burned/damaged. No fire, no injury.
root_cause: Solid-state relay (SSR) in SB-2 temperature controller failed in closed (conducting) position — SSR latched on, bypassing the temperature controller's PID loop.
resolution: SSR replaced. All seal bar SSRs on Line 1 and 2 added to annual PM list. High-limit cutout verified functional before restart. 80 damaged units scrapped.

title: TFB Line 3 — FIFO Violation, Mixed Lots
description: Audit found two raw material lots intermixed on Line 3 staging area. Lot travelers missing from one pallet. Unable to confirm material identity for approximately 300 kg of raw stock.
root_cause: Forklift operator placed new pallet in front of existing pallet in staging area, violating FIFO. Missing traveler pallet came from emergency material transfer last night shift — traveler left with the shipper.
resolution: Unidentified material quarantined. Supplier contacted for lot certification re-issuance. FIFO marking system installed in staging area (floor paint, stack-order arrows). Traveler requirements emphasized in pre-shift meetings.

title: SAP Line A — Press P-2 Die Spring Failure
description: Stripper plate on Press P-2 not returning properly. Parts showing deformation from incomplete stripping. 1.5 hours downtime, 200 nonconforming parts.
root_cause: Stripper plate spring (Part No. SP-2204) fractured — metallurgical analysis showed fatigue crack initiating at spring coil end. Spring had 18 months service; rated replacement interval 12 months in die card.
resolution: Spring replaced. Die card PM schedule corrected. All other P-2 stripper springs inspected — two additional showing incipient cracks, replaced proactively.

title: BPK Line 2 — CB-3 Belt Misalignment
description: CB-3 belt tracking to right side, wearing belt edge against frame. Detected by operator; belt not yet damaged. Corrected in 20 minutes.
root_cause: Drive pulley lagging worn asymmetrically — right side lower coefficient of friction than left, pulling belt off-center.
resolution: Drive pulley lagging replaced. Belt tension re-set per STD-001. No product impact.

title: TFB Line B — C-12 Throughput Drop, Suspected Bearing
description: 18% throughput drop on TFB Line B since 06:00. Operators reporting vibration. Maintenance investigating.
root_cause: Under investigation at time of record creation.
resolution: Pending.

title: SAP Line C — Jenner Valve Lot, Cpk 1.28 (Conditional)
description: Latest Jenner Valve lot passed minimum bar for conditional approval (Cpk 1.28, above 1.33... wait, 1.28 < 1.33). Lot rejected. Fourth SCAR in 15 months.
root_cause: Supplier audit (Q4 2024) revealed: no real-time SPC on machining center, tool change intervals not data-driven, quality tech position vacant for 3 months.
resolution: Lot rejected. Sourcing from backup supplier SUP-DM extended. Supplier placed on Controlled Shipping Level 2 (100% inspection + source inspection). Formal improvement plan required by Jan 31.

title: BPK Line 1 — 25% Throughput Drop, Label Application Issue
description: Throughput dropped 25% on BPK Line 1 at 08:30. Label application station applying labels skewed >3 mm. 600 units rejected, 200 reworked.
root_cause: Label roll core diameter varied: new roll lot had core ID 0.5 mm smaller than spec, causing misfit on applicator mandrel and slight off-axis run-out.
resolution: Incoming inspection added for label core diameter. Lot returned to label supplier. Two acceptable lots identified in inventory, production restored at 10:15.

title: TFB Line 1 — Conveyor C-01 Drive Belt Failure
description: C-01 V-belt drive failed at 11:20. Belt sheave failure — belt thrown. 25-minute unplanned downtime.
root_cause: V-belt sheave groove worn beyond tolerance, causing belt to ride low and generate excess heat. Sheave not included in PM scope.
resolution: Belt and sheave replaced. V-belt sheave added to PM checklist for all conveyors.

title: SAP Line B — Operator Injury, Laceration
description: Operator suffered laceration on right hand at assembly station 4 when fixture guard was removed for cleaning and not replaced before resuming production. OSHA recordable.
root_cause: Guard removal procedure did not specify that production must stop before guard replacement. Operator attempted to clean fixture while line was running.
resolution: Stop work. EHS investigation. Guard interlocked with line enable. Procedure updated: guards are safety-critical devices, line must be stopped before removal. All station 4 operators retrained.

title: BPK Line 3 — 30% Throughput Drop, Seal + Labeling
description: Throughput on Line 3 dropped 30% starting 07:00. Both seal integrity failures (SB3 temp at 135°C) and label skew occurring simultaneously.
root_cause: Line 3 underwent extended changeover previous shift (3.5 hours). Seal bar temperature controller setpoint was reset to a test value during changeover and not restored. Label applicator mandrel was also not re-secured post-changeover.
resolution: Seal bar reset to 150°C. Mandrel secured. Added changeover checklist signoff for seal bar setpoint and label station configuration. 320 units scrapped.

title: TFB Line 2 — Press P-5 SPC Excursion, Part Weight
description: SPC control chart for part weight showed 3 consecutive points beyond 2σ above mean (Nelson Rule 2). Process paused. Assignable cause found in 22 minutes.
root_cause: Raw material lot switch mid-run: new lot from backup supplier SUP-DM had slightly higher density (material cert in tolerance but upper quartile). Weight shift was real, not measurement error.
resolution: SPC baseline recalculated for new material lot. Process parameters adjusted. Control charts updated with new baseline. No quality hold required — all parts within specification.

title: SAP Line A — Conveyor C-05 Bearing Failure, High Vibration
description: Vibration alarm on C-05 at 09:30. Measured 7.3 mm/s RMS (Zone D — stop immediately per STD-010). Line stopped. Bearing outer race cracked.
root_cause: C-05 bearing was from same non-OEM suspect lot as C-12 (INC-2024-003) and C-03 (INC-2024-011). Not identified in the proactive audit because C-05 was on a different sub-inventory list.
resolution: Bearing replaced with OEM SKF part. Final audit of all facilities for remaining non-OEM lot bearings completed. Four more found and replaced. Lot fully eliminated from inventory.

title: BPK Line 2 — Sanitation Compliance Verification Hold
description: QA walk-through found sanitation log for BPK Line 2 not signed off for last shift (Friday afternoon). Line idle since then (68 hours). ATP swab taken before QA cleared — result 380 RLU (limit 100). Pre-production sanitation performed.
root_cause: Friday operator finished sanitation steps but forgot to sign log. Subsequent shift assumed sanitation was not done (correctly interpreted unsigned log as not done) and held the line for QA.
resolution: Full sanitation completed. ATP confirmed 42 RLU. System worked as intended — log requirement caught the gap. Operator counseled on documentation requirement.

title: TFB Line 3 — Backup Supplier Lot Dimensional Issue
description: Backup supplier SUP-DM lot for rod stock measured 0.03 mm undersize on OD (tolerance ±0.025 mm). AQL sample: 3 of 50 nonconforming. Lot quarantined.
root_cause: SUP-DM was expedited to higher volumes than normal; grinding operation not recalibrated for increased feed rate.
resolution: Lot returned. SCAR-2025-004 issued to SUP-DM. Inspection 100% on next two lots. Existing stock from SUP-DM on other lines inspected — all conforming. Previous lots (different feed rate) unaffected.

title: SAP Line B — 20% Throughput Drop, Morning Shift
description: Throughput on SAP Line B dropped 20% at shift start (06:00). No obvious noise. Operators report conveyor C-08 feeling sluggish. Maintenance called at 06:15.
root_cause: Under investigation.
resolution: Pending.

title: BPK Line 1 — Seal Bar SB-1 Low Temperature Warning
description: SB-1 temperature alarm at 07:45: reading 138°C (setpoint 150°C, limit 140°C per STD-012). Production paused. Three test packs failed peel strength (hot-tack 9 N/15mm, limit 12 N). ~150 units on hold.
root_cause: Under investigation. Thermocouple replaced 6 months ago (INC-2024-006); this may be SSR or heater element issue.
resolution: Pending.
